title: Earth Brain Bidirectional Event Flow Schema
description: Schema for defining bidirectional event flows across Earth Brain OS layers in v0.4.

flow_id.description: Unique identifier for this bidirectional flow record.

version.description: Version of the bidirectional flow schema or model.

flow_model.description: Canonical name of the bidirectional flow model.

earth_brain_event_id.description: Reference to the Earth Brain event associated with this flow.

lifecycle_id.description: Reference to the lifecycle record associated with this flow.

flow_purpose.description: Human-readable purpose of this bidirectional event flow.
examples: Define how a human prompt is processed by the AI Agent Layer and returned through trace, review, governance, and human feedback paths.

active_direction.description: Current dominant direction of the event flow.

participating_layers.description: Earth Brain OS layers participating in this bidirectional flow.

flow_edges.description: Directed edges representing event movement between layers.

edge_id.description: Unique identifier for this flow edge.

direction.description: Direction of the event flow edge.

source_layer.description: Layer where this flow edge starts.

target_layer.description: Layer where this flow edge ends.

signal_type.description: Type of signal or event moving through this edge.

condition.description: Condition under which this flow edge is activated.
examples: AI agent completes trace-aware reasoning and requests human review.

trace_required.description: Whether this edge requires trace recording.

review_required.description: Whether this edge requires review before continuation.

lifecycle_state_hint.description: Optional lifecycle state associated with this edge.

notes.description: Optional notes about this flow edge.

return_paths.description: Return paths that carry review, feedback, regulation, defense, or governance signals back toward human or AI layers.

path_id.description: Unique identifier for this return path.

from_layer.description: Layer where the return path starts.

to_layer.description: Layer where the return path ends.

trigger.description: Trigger condition for this return path.
examples: Human review requests clarification from the AI Agent Layer.

requires_human_attention.description: Whether this return path requires explicit human attention.

trace_reference.description: Optional reference to a trace or review record.

review_boundaries.description: Review boundaries that constrain bidirectional event movement.

human_review_on_return_required.description: Whether return flows to the human layer require human review.

governance_review_on_policy_change_requi.description: Whether policy-changing flows require governance review.

defense_review_on_risk_required.description: Whether risky flows require defense review.

trace_required_for_all_edges.description: Whether all flow edges require trace records.

automatic_forward_flow_allowed.description: Whether forward flow may proceed automatically when validation passes.

automatic_return_flow_allowed.description: Whether return flow may proceed automatically without human review.

flow_summary.description: Optional summary of the bidirectional event flow.

forward_path.description: Human-readable summary of the forward path.

return_path.description: Human-readable summary of the return path.

feedback_loop_enabled.description: Whether this flow supports iterative feedback loops.

status.description: Operational status of this bidirectional flow record.

notes.description: Optional notes about this bidirectional flow record.
